title: Delicate Healing Polish Turkey Mince Dumplings
description: A traditional Polish dish featuring turkey mince and herbs, designed to help alleviate hangover symptoms with its anti-inflammatory properties. This recipe utilizes an ice bath to shock the dumplings, making them delicate and shattering.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the turkey mince, chopped onion, minced garlic, chopped parsley, chopped dill, beaten egg, salt, and black pepper, mixing well with your hands or a wooden spoon until just combined.
Cover the mixture and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
When ready to cook, bring the chicken broth to a boil in a large pot.
Using your hands, shape the turkey mixture into small dumplings, about 1 1/2 inches in diameter, making sure to handle them gently to avoid compacting the meat.
Gently add the dumplings to the boiling broth, being careful not to overcrowd the pot.
Once the dumplings float to the surface, cook for an additional 5 minutes, then immediately transfer them to an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Let the dumplings sit in the ice bath for 5 minutes to shock them and make them delicate and shattering.
Remove the dumplings from the ice bath and serve hot, garnished with additional chopped parsley and dill if desired.
Traditionally, these dumplings are served in the hot chicken broth, but you can also serve them with a side of rye bread or boiled potatoes for a more filling meal.
